blockers.description: Need Pi eth0 connected to projector to test ADCP commands
blockers.workaround: Direct ethernet cable between Pi and projector

human_actions_pending.action: Connect ethernet cable from Pi to projector
human_actions_pending.context: Need network path for ADCP TCP:53595
nmcli shared mode gives projector an IP via DHCP
Config with discovered scancodes and ADCP commands

human_actions_pending.action: Verify projector has Network Standby enabled

decisions.decision: Dispatch on EV_MSC/MSC_SCAN directly, not EV_KEY
decisions.rationale: Without keymap loaded, kernel only emits MSC_SCAN events

decisions.decision: power_toggle queries power_status before sending on/off
decisions.rationale: Remote has single power button but ADCP needs explicit on/off

decisions.decision: Brightness/contrast/sharpness up/down may need query-then-set pattern
decisions.rationale: ADCP uses absolute values, not relative increments — needs testing

next_action: Connect ethernet from Pi to projector, configure nmcli shared mode on eth0, find projector IP, create config, run bridge mode
context_notes: Phase 3 code is complete and verified. Hardware testing on Pi confirmed IR reception works with KY-022 sensor. Discover mode working — all 33 remote button scancodes mapped. Now need to connect projector via ethernet and test ADCP commands end-to-end. ADCP commands sourced from Bcukier/sony_projector_adcp GitHub repo. Some commands (brightness/contrast relative adjustments, aspect, 3D, color space) still need discovery against actual projector.
